mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively low consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL < 1.5%) signals deflationary risks that prompt central bank intervention, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over 60 trading days through liquidity injections.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain negative in 70%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL < 1.5%, or inflation expectations (TIPS spread) do not widen by >50bps.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real personal consumption (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust economic demand, creating positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through earnings optimism.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative in 60%+ of periods where both RSAFS and INDPRO show positive momentum, or their combined signal rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply chain disruptions that raise corporate input costs, driving S&P 500 forward returns negatively over 5 trading days through profit margin compression.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 70%+ of instances where INDPRO declines > 2% monthly, or commodity prices (CRB) do not rise by >5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden acceleration in payroll employment (PAYEMS) beyond 200k new jobs signals labor market overheating, prompting Federal Reserve rate hikes that drive S&P 500 forward returns negatively over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200k, or FEDFUNDS does not rise by >0.5% in the subsequent month.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.2 million units indicates weak consumer confidence, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through reduced wealth effect and investment sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where HOUST < 1.2M, or leading economic index (LEI) does not decline by >1%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and stagnant money supply growth (RSAFS) indicates monetary policy ineffectiveness, driving S&P 500 forward returns negatively over 20 trading days through stagflation risks.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds RSAFS, or velocity of money (M2/GDP) does not decline by >10%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in unemployment (UNRATE) and rise in wage growth (PAYEMS) signals tight labor market dynamics that force corporate cost-push inflation, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 3.5% and PAYEMS > 150k, or unit labor costs (CPIAUCSL/PAYEMS) do not rise by >0.8%.
